Pharmacist
RPh, PharmD Guide

Issued by the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission under the Washington State Department of Health (DOH). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$250
$250 application/license fee
Renewal
$150
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 6 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission

Fax: N/A (contact by phone)

Email

See board website

Credential Categories

Washington Pharmacist License Types

RPh, PharmD

The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Washington.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Washington RPh, PharmD

Complete Education Requirements

Complete an ACPE-accredited Doctor of Pharmacy (PharmD) program and required pharmacy internship hours.

Pass the Board Exams

Successfully pass the expected board examinations, typically the NAPLEX and MPJE.

Submit Application and Fees

Complete the state application, undergo a background check, and submit the necessary licensing fees.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official TranscriptsSent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score ReportsOfficial verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

Frequently Asked Questions

Washington Pharmacist FAQ

How much does a Washington pharmacist license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

Is the MPJE required in all states?

Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.
